content: Autonomous curiosity mechanisms from curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data samples get stored and consolidated into a 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent's long-term memory, cutting memory bloat while improving downstream task generalization. This creates a self-sustaining cycle where the agent's intrinsic motivation to explore automatically curates its memory for active learning, eliminating the need for hand-designed memory pruning rules.
